ORDER TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND (VACATED PER ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATON FILED 1/11/13) DEFAULT OF MAY-03-2010 SET ASIDE, JUDGMENT OF MAY-03-2010 SET ASIDE

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SERVPRO ROHILLCO BUSINESS SERVICES' MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION IS CONTINUED ON COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JANUARY 11, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 302 BY JUDGE KAHN. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON II; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF SERVPRO ROHILLCO BUSINESS SERVICES' MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION - THE MOTION IS GRANTED. THE PRIOR ORDER IS VACATED AND THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARE REINSTATED. THE PRIOR ORDER STATED THAT RELIEF WAS GRANTED PER CCP 437(D), WHICH REFERS TO VOID JUDGMENTS. ALTHOUGH THE PRIOR ORDER DID NOT STATE WHETHER THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT WERE VOID ON THEIR FACE OR FACIALLY VALID BUT VOID FOR LACK OF SERVICE, THE PRIOR ORDER DID STATE THAT DEFENDANT "PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT SHE WAS NOT PROPERLY SERVED." THIS LANGUAGE CLEARLY SHOWS THAT I DETERMINED THAT THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT WERE VOID FOR LACK OF SERVICE BECAUSE A FACIALLY VOID ORDER IS DETERMINED SOLELY BY LOOKING AT THE PROOF OF SERVICE AND DOES NOT CONSIDER THE ASSERTEDLY SERVED PARTY'S EVIDENCE. A MOTION TO SET ASIDE A DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT BASED ON THE GROUNDS THAT THEY ARE FACIALLY VALID BUT VOID FOR LACK OF SERVICE MUST BE FILED WITHIN TWO YEARS OF THE ENTRY OF THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT. HERE, THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT WAS ENTERED ON MAY 3, 2010, BUT THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE WAS NOT FILED UNTIL JULY 6, 2012, WHICH MAKES THE MOTION UNTIMELY. RECONSIDERATION IS WARRANTED UNDER CCP 1008 BECAUSE NEITHER AT THE HEARING NOR IN THE PARTIES' PAPERS WAS ANY DISTINCTION MADE BETWEEN A FACIALLY VOID JUDGMENT AND A FACIALLY VALID JUDGMENT WHICH IS VOID DUE TO IMPROPER SERVICE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: LAVENNA WARD, CSR # 7077

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Apr-30-2013 Discharged

Courts Motion To Dismiss Appeal
Off Calendar Apr-23-2013 Granted Per Written Order

Motion For Reconsideration
Off Calendar Jan-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEVEN BOOSKA, TEL. NO. APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NOELL KUBOTA OF THE LAW OFFICES OF KUBOTA & CONSTANTINO, TEL. NO. (650) 579-7535 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SERVPRO ROHILLCO BUSINESS SERVICES' Motion For Reconsideration - The motion is granted. The prior order is vacated and the default and default judgment are reinstated. The prior order stated that relief was granted per CCP 437(d), which refers to void judgments. Although the prior order did not state whether the default and default judgment were void on their face or facially valid but void for lack of service, the prior order did state that defendant "presented sufficient evidence to show that she was not properly served." This language clearly shows that I determined that the default and default judgment were void for lack of service because a facially void order is determined solely by looking at the proof of service and does not consider the assertedly served party's evidence. A motion to set aside a default and default judgment based on the grounds that they are facially valid but void for lack of service must be filed within two years of the entry of the default and default judgment. Here, the default judgment was entered on May 3, 2010, but the motion to set aside was not filed until July 6, 2012, which makes the motion untimely. Reconsideration is warranted under CCP 1008 because neither at the hearing nor in the parties' papers was any distinction made between a facially void judgment and a facially valid judgment which is void due to improper service. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVenna Ward, CSR # 7077
